subject: Statistics
title: Bayesian A/B Testing
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Bayesian A/B Testing.

hook.text: Frequentist A/B tests need a fixed sample size decided in advance. Bayesian tests can stop anytime.

buildup.text: Bayesian A/B testing updates the probability that A is better than B after each new data point.

discovery.text: You can peek at results daily without inflating error rates. The posterior is valid at any stopping point.

twist.text: This flexibility comes at a cost: you need to specify priors, and results depend on those choices.

climax.text: Tech companies increasingly prefer Bayesian A/B tests for their flexibility and intuitive probability outputs.

punchline.text: Stop when you're confident. No fixed sample required.

quiz.question: What advantage does Bayesian A/B testing have over frequentist approaches?
options: You can check results at any time without inflating false positive rates
It requires no data at all
It's always faster regardless of the effect size
